definition: The US 6.4% annualized real return is an outlier in a 35-country sample where the median is ~3-4% (Dimson-Marsh-Staunton 2024). Generalizing US equity cycle patterns globally is a specific and testable error. Multiple developed markets experienced decade-plus negative real return periods.
evidence: Japan, Italy, Austria experienced decade-plus negative real return periods
Median cross-country real return of ~3-4% vs US 6.4%
open_questions: Whether US institutional advantages justify higher structural returns
Whether US exceptionalism is persistent or mean-reverting
Implications for global equity allocation frameworks
